label: yes
cot_prefix: Okay, let's try to figure this out. So Jane was born on the last day of February in 2001. Wait, February's last day is usually the 28th, unless it's a leap year when it's the 29th. But 2001 isn't a leap year because leap years are divisible by 4. 2000 was a leap year, then 2004. So 2001's February had 28 days. So Jane's birthday is February 28, 2001. Today is her 16th birthday. So that means today's date is February 28, 2017 (since 2001 + 16 = 2017). Now, the question is asking for the date 10 days ago from today. So we need to subtract 10 days from February 28, 2017. Let me count backwards. Starting from February 28, 2017: - 1 day before is Feb 27 - 2 days: Feb 26 - 3: 25 - 4:24 - 5:23 - 6:22 - 7:21 - 8:20 - 9:19 - 10 days ago would be Feb 18, 2017? Wait, but wait. Wait, if today is Feb 28, 2017, then 10 days before that would be subtracting 10 days. Let me check if 2017 is a leap year? 2017 divided by 4 is 504.25, so not a leap year. So February has 28 days. So if I subtract 10 days from Feb 28, 2017: Feb 28 minus 10 days. Let's think: if I go back 28 - 10 = 18. So that would be Feb 18, 2017. But wait, if the 28th minus 1 day is the 27th, then minus 10 days would be the 18th. So yes, that would be option B: 02/18/2017. But let me double check. Alternatively, sometimes when you subtract days, you might have to consider if you cross into another month, but since we're subtracting 10 days from the 28th, and February has 28 days in 2017, it's straightforward. 28 - 10 = 18. So the date is 02/18/2017. So option B. But let me make sure I didn't miscalculate. Let me count each day: Starting from today, Feb 28, 2017 (her 16th birthday). Let's count 10 days prior: Day 1: Feb 27 Day 2: Feb 26 Day 3: Feb 25 Day 4: Feb 24 Day 5: Feb 23 Day 6: Feb 22 Day 7: Feb 21 Day 8: Feb 20 Day 9: Feb
